items.title: Food security drive gains RM559.85m boost as Hajiji ties Kaamatan spirit to harvest resilience
items.summary: PAPAR, May 1 — The Sabah state government has allocated a total of RM559.85 million to the state’s Ministry of Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry (MAFFI) this year to improve food security and the self-sufficiency rate (SSR). Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said various efforts have also been and are being implemented by the government to ensure food security in the state, including the development of padi fields throughout the state, including in Papar and surrounding districts such as Membakut, Beaufort and Kuala Penyu. “In line with the spirit of the Kaamatan Festival which values the harvest, the agriculture sector remains one of the three main pillars that drive the state’s economic growth,” he said at the launching of Sabah state-level Kaamatan Festival here today.

items.title: Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm
items.summary: MET0105 Thunderstorm alert issued across multiple states until 4pm Caption MetMalaysia warns of heavy rain and strong winds in short-term advisory. – Source: Malaysian Meteorological Dept New Straits Times KUALA LUMPUR: The Meteorological Department has issued a thunderstorm warning for several states, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until 4pm today. The alert, issued at 1.15pm, covers parts of Kedah, Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Selangor, Negri Sembilan, Melaka and Johor, as well as the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya.

items.title: UK police charge man with stabbing attack on two Jewish Londoners
items.summary: LONDON: UK police said on Friday they had charged a 45-year-old man over the stabbing of two Jewish men in north London, the latest in a string of hate attacks. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed Thursday to boost security for the Jewish community after accusations from angry British Jews that his government has repeatedly failed to protect them. The two men were attacked in broad daylight in Golders Green, a north London area with a large Jewish population.

items.title: Foreigners begging at KLIA arrested after videos go viral
items.summary: SEPANG: Two foreign men were arrested at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) today for allegedly begging for money to cover their daily expenses. The suspects, a 28-year-old Tajikistani and a 45-year-old Palestinian, are United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cardholders. Klia police chief Assistant Commissioner M. Ravi said the arrests followed a complaint from Malaysia Airports Aviation Security (Avsec).

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia working to free detained Global Sumud activists
items.summary: RAWANG: Malaysia is working with other countries to secure the release of Global Sumud Flotilla activists detained by Israel, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar said many of the activists were detained in international waters, describing Israel's actions as a "mad act" that disregards international principles. "They were still in international waters," he said, adding that some activists had been forced to move into Greek waters, where their safety was guaranteed by the Greek government.

items.title: Blossom Wong, ex-Special Branch and undercover operative, dies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Blossom Wong, one of Malaysia's most well-known Special Branch officers and undercover operatives, has died. Her death was confirmed by her daughter, Dr Christina Blossom Welch who recieved the news around 9am. Dr Christina said her mother's time in the force never weakened the bond she had with her children.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan Umno wings push back, call state’s Balai Undang Luak directive unfair and heavy‑handed
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The Negeri Sembilan Umno Wanita, Youth and Puteri wings have expressed concern over the relocation of staff from the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong and the vacating of its official residence, describing the move as insensitive in light of an ongoing customary dispute. In a joint statement, the three wings said they took a “serious view” of the directive issued to relocate all residents at the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong, followed by an instruction to vacate both the official residence and the premises on April 29, 2026. “It is clear that this directive was issued while the Balai Undang Luak Sungei Ujong was managing an unresolved dispute involving YTM Datuk Klana Petra Undang Luak Sungei Ujong.

items.title: Iran activates air defences as Trump faces congressional deadline
items.summary: TEHERAN: Teheran's air defences were activated to counter small aircraft and drones late Thursday, as the White House signalled that it will not be reined in by a congressional deadline on the Iran war. US President Donald Trump's administration faced a looming midnight deadline to secure congressional authorisation for the war against Iran, setting up a clash between the White House and Congress. 'SHAMEFUL DEFEAT' Earlier on Thursday, Iran's supreme leader declared that the United States had suffered a shameful defeat, defiantly rejecting a warning from Trump that an economically punishing US naval blockade could be enforced for months to come.

items.title: Anwar urges calm in Negri Sembilan political crisis
items.summary: RAWANG: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged all parties in Negri Sembilan to allow the state's political situation to cool down. Anwar said the matter should be handled in accordance with the state constitution and relevant laws. "Negri Sembilan, let the situation cool down first.

items.title: Anwar says no more waiting, workers’ initiatives must hit ministers’ desks in two weeks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has given all relevant agencies a two-week deadline to submit reports on the implementation of each workers’ initiative announced today. Today, the announcement is made; within two weeks the report must be on the minister’s desk, and must be presented for Cabinet consideration within three weeks,” he said. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Human Resource Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, Cabinet members, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, senior government officials, as well as representatives of workers’ unions and employers.

items.title: Storm rips roofs off schools, damages 90 homes in Seberang Perai Utara
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS: Four schools in Seberang Perai Utara were damaged after a storm with strong winds struck late yesterday evening, with one school suffering significant roof damage. The affected schools are Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan (SMK) Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir, SMK Gemilang, SMK Permatang Tok Jaya and Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Hashim Awang. Penang Education Department director Mohamad Dziauddin Mat Saad said SMK Datuk Haji Abdul Kadir was the worst hit, with part of the roof of a block housing five classrooms torn off by strong winds.

items.title: Penang picked for Indian Ocean maritime heritage Unesco bid involving 17 countries
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 1 — Penang has been selected to participate in the Indian government’s initiative for a transnational Indian Ocean maritime Unesco listing. Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the initiative involves 17 countries around the Indian Ocean, including Malaysia. He said the maritime aspect of the Indian Ocean is not the sole prerogative of the Indian subcontinent.

items.title: Mexico demands evidence behind US drug charges against governor
items.summary: MEXICO CITY: Mexico is seeking "irrefutable" evidence to back up the shock US drug trafficking charges against a sitting governor and other officials before proceeding with extradition requests, President Claudia Sheinbaum said Thursday. The US Justice Department unveiled charges on Wednesday against Sinaloa Governor Ruben Rocha Moya and nine others, accusing them of working with the notorious Sinaloa cartel to distribute "massive quantities" of narcotics to the United States. The other officials facing US charges are also part of Morena.

items.title: 1 in 10 Malaysians aged 60 and above living with dementia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is facing a growing public health challenge, with about 10 per cent of citizens aged 60 and above living with dementia, according to the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS) 2025. The coalition comprises the Dementia Alliance of Malaysia, Alzheimer's Disease Foundation Malaysia, Johor Bahru Alzheimer's Disease Support Association, Dementia Society Perak and Penang Dementia Association. Tan said improvements in healthy ageing indicators, including lower depression rates and better daily functioning among older adults, remain insufficient to curb the rise in dementia.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow announces late fee exemptions as Penang land tax review sparks over 10,000 appeals
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, May 1 — The Penang State Government has extended the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the new quit rent (land tax) review until May 31, said Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow. Chow said the decision was made through the State Executive Council (MMK) meeting dated April 29, which agreed to extend the appeal period for cases arising from the implementation of the new land tax review until May 31. He said the state government hoped that with the exemption from land tax, parcel tax and temporary block tax after May 31, it would give all landowners the opportunity to pay their land tax as soon as possible and avoid arrears on their tax accounts by 2027.

items.title: RTM, unions and media figures shine at Labour Day Awards 2026
items.summary: In the media category, the Best Individual Media Award 2026 was presented to Terence Tan Janmoy of Star Media Group. Among the awards presented were the National Labour Figure Award (Public Service), the Best National Trade Union Award for the public, private and statutory body sectors, as well as the Best Media Award covering individual and agency categories. In the trade union category, the Best National Trade Union Award (Public Sector) 2026 was awarded to the Sarawak Teachers' Union (STU).

items.title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
items.summary: Gold prices were largely steady in thin trading on Friday, but were headed for a weekly decline, pressured by higher oil prices that have stoked inflation concerns and reinforced expectations of higher-for-longer interest rates. US gold futures for June delivery fell 0.1 per cent to US$4,626.40. In other metals, spot silver rose 0.4 per cent to US$73.99 per ounce, platinum eased 0.2 per cent to US$1,981.25 and palladium added 0.1 per cent to US$1,525.36.

items.title: Fuel subsidies keep lid on transport costs, but Tengku Zafrul warns supply crunch will test households
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The impact of the global supply crisis on the cost of living is expected to begin to be felt by Malaysians within one to two months, said senior political advisor to the Prime Minister Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz. He said this was driven by increased costs in the supply chain including logistics, fertiliser and other industrial inputs. Tengku Zafrul said among the sectors expected to be affected first are logistics, followed by agriculture and industries that rely on raw materials such as plastic.

items.title: Anwar pushes for fairer wages, telling corporate Malaysia to match profits with compassion for workers
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on employers to be more considerate and compassionate in ensuring the welfare of workers continues to be protected. He said peace and rapid national development must be matched by guarantees of well-being for all segments of society, including workers. That is the priority,” he said at the national-level 2026 Workers’ Day celebration here today.

items.title: Viral 'orangutan cuddling leopard' image is AI-generated, says DGFC
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A viral image purportedly showing an orangutan cuddling a clouded leopard is not genuine, according to the Danau Girang Field Centre (DGFC). The Sabah-based research facility said the image, widely circulated on Facebook, was likely generated using artificial intelligence (AI). While the image appeared heartwarming, DGFC said it depicted a biological impossibility.

items.title: bzBee bags two wins at Davos Communications Awards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian consultancy bzBee Consult has won two awards at the Davos Communications Awards 2026, marking its third consecutive year of recognition on the international stage. The agency clinched Best Lifestyle Campaign for Meowgical Malaysia Project – A Book of Poems: The Meowgical Tails of Malaysia and Sustainability Communications Campaign for Indigenous Wisdom, Youth Voices, Global Action: The RAYS 2025 Legacy. Meanwhile, the Meowgical Malaysia project, developed with the Ministry of Tourism Sarawak, won for its creative approach to tourism storytelling through a series of poems set across Malaysia's 13 states.

items.title: Indonesia to cut ride-hailing companies' maximum commission taken from drivers to 8pct
items.summary: Here are the details: * Prabowo said he signed a presidential regulation which was the basis for the new maximum commission cap that ride-hailing firms could take from their driver partners. * Prabowo did not say when the regulation would take effect. * Aside from the lower commission cap, the regulation will also require ride-hailing firms to provide accident and health insurance, Prabowo said.

items.title: Govt won't betray people's mandate, says Anwar amid rising oil prices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said his administration will not betray the people's mandate as it takes economic measures to manage rising oil prices driven by the conflict in the Middle East. Anwar said that while the government is maintaining the price of RON95 at RM1.99 per litre, further measures may be necessary as the global economic outlook is expected to deteriorate. Anwar said economists and corporate groups have suggested fuel price hikes are necessary to safeguard the economy, but he remains mindful of the impact on lower-income workers.

items.title: Average formal sector wage rises to RM3,167 in December 2025, says DOSM
items.summary: In comparison, female employees, who make up 44.9 per cent (3.18 million), recorded a median wage of RM3,120. In terms of ethnicity, Bumiputera formal employees recorded a median monthly wage of RM2,627, while Chinese employees earned RM4,767. Indian employees recorded a median wage of RM3,120 in December 2025.

items.title: Stray livestock continue to claim lives, cause millions in losses in Gua Musang
items.summary: GUA MUSANG, May 1 (Bernama) – The lives of road users continue to be at stake as livestock roam freely along main roads in this district, with hundreds of accidents recorded annually and losses reaching millions of ringgit. Gua Musang district officer Mohd Roshdi Ismail said that throughout 2024, a total of 191 accidents involving cattle were recorded, with estimated losses exceeding RM1.14 million. – MORE STRAY LIVESTOCK-LIFE 3 GUA MUSANG "In 2024, a total of 11 cattle were auctioned and eight in 2025," said Mohd Roshdi, who is also Gua Musang District Council president.

items.title: Powerwell may post robust earnings growth: Affin Hwang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Powerwell Holdings Bhd is expected to deliver robust earnings growth over the next few years, underpinned by rising demand from data centres and renewable energy projects. "This will be driven by the surging data centre pipeline, overseas renewable energy projects, strategic capacity expansion, and value-accretive acquisitions," it said in a note. Affin Hwang said demand from data centre and corporate transformation initiatives is expected to drive growth for Powerwell.

items.title: Expert warns of human risk from monkey malaria in Sabah amid high cases
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: A medical expert has called for measures to protect people living in Sabah's forested areas, citing the high rate of monkey malaria cases in the state, which account for half of the cases nationwide. Quoting data from the Health Ministry and Sabah Health Department, Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) medicine professor Dr Jenarun Jelip said while local transmission of human malaria was no longer a risk in Malaysia, the threat of monkey malaria remained. He said of the 2,063 monkey malaria cases nationwide, a total of 1,154 were recorded in Sabah.

items.title: Anwar rolls out allowance plan, giving mothers 80pc salary support for longer recovery after childbirth
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The government will introduce the Post-Maternity Leave Allowance as a progressive measure to support women’s participation in the workforce and reduce the stress faced by mothers after giving birth. He said the allowance would be introduced through an amendment to the Employment Insurance System Act 2017 and is aimed at providing additional financial support to mothers who needed longer time after maternity leave. Anwar said the initiative is expected to benefit more than 132,000 female workers nationwide.

items.title: Dunlop charts reset in Malaysia as Toyotsu Binter takes the wheel
items.summary: Dunlop is repositioning its presence in Malaysia with a renewed market strategy anchored by a network-driven expansion under its new exclusive distributor Toyotsu Binter Malaysia Sdn Bhd. The reset, unveiled at Malaysia Autoshow 2026 in Penang, marks the brand's first major public outing since Toyotsu Binter's appointment earlier this year. "This marks the beginning of a new chapter for Dunlop in Malaysia," Toyotsu Binter managing director Marcus Lim.

items.title: US house approves DHS funding bill to end record 75-day shutdown
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States (US) House of Representatives on Thursday approved a Senate-passed funding bill that would finance much of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), in an effort to end the agency's record 75-day shutdown, reported Xinhua. The bill will reopen the DHS without allocating new funding for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or the US Border Patrol, as Democrats have been calling for major reforms to immigration enforcement following two fatal shootings of US citizens by federal immigration agents in January. Against that backdrop, DHS funding expired on Feb, 13.

items.title: Protesters clash with Australian police after suspected killer of Indigenous girl arrested
items.summary: SYDNEY: Hundreds of protesters clashed with Australian emergency services workers in a remote town following the arrest of a man suspected of murdering a five-year-old Indigenous girl, police said on Friday. Jefferson Lewis, a 47-year-old man who police say they believe abducted and killed the girl, was found by locals and beaten unconscious on Thursday night before police apprehended him, Northern Territory Police Commissioner Martin Dole said at a news conference. "He presented himself to one of the town camps in Alice Springs last night.

items.title: HKL says ‘war zone’ viral claim due to ongoing emergency department upgrade works
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR — Hospital Kuala Lumpur (HKL) has clarified that images circulating on social media depicting its Emergency and Trauma Department as a “war zone” since Wednesday are due to ongoing upgrading works. HKL director Datuk Dr Harikrishna K R Nair said the project is a strategic plan to enhance patient capacity and improve service comfort. “Despite space constraints, healthcare services at the Emergency and Trauma Department continue as usual under the ‘no wrong door’ principle,” he said in a statement.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar urges bosses to raise wages with compassion
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged corporate leaders to show greater compassion and responsibility by raising wages to safeguard employee welfare and boost productivity. "We want disciplined and highly productive workers. Anwar said at the National Labour Day celebration.

items.title: Pos Malaysia names former Telekom Malaysia chief, Communications Ministry sec-gen as board directors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pos Malaysia Bhd has strengthened its board with the appointment of two non-independent non-executive directors and an alternate director effective May 1. Pos Malaysia said he previously served as managing director/group chief executive officer of Telekom Malaysia Bhd. Pos Malaysia said Abdul Halim brings three decades of distinguished public sector experience, with deep expertise in governance, policy and national-level administration.

items.title: Azizan Abdul Aziz named Bursa Malaysia's islamic capital market director
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia Bhd has appointed Azizan Abdul Aziz as director of its Islamic capital market (ICM) effective today. Bursa Malaysia chief executive officer Datuk Fad'l Mohamed said the appointment reflects Bursa Malaysia's continued focus on strengthening its Islamic capital market proposition alongside its broader strategic priorities. According to the statement, Malaysia's Islamic capital market remains a key pillar of the domestic capital market, having expanded from RM2.2 trillion in 2020 to RM2.7 trillion by end-2025, with shariah-compliant equities and sukuk contributing RM1.3 trillion and RM1.4 trillio, respectively.

items.title: US official says Iran war truce 'terminated' hostilities for war powers deadline
items.summary: WASHINGTON: A US-Iran ceasefire that began in early April has "terminated" hostilities between the two sides for the purposes of an approaching congressional war powers deadline, a senior official of President Donald Trump's administration said on Thursday. "For War Powers Resolution purposes, the hostilities that began on Saturday, February 28, have terminated," said the official, describing the administration's thinking. The Iran war began with airstrikes launched by Israel and the United States on February 28.

items.title: JPJ denies viral claims of free driving licence offer, warns public of fake ads
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 1 — The Road Transport Department (JPJ) has denied claims circulating on social media, including Threads, alleging that free driving licences are being offered. JPJ stressed that the advertisements are false and invalid. “The public is encouraged to always verify information through official JPJ channels before believing or sharing any information.

items.title: HKL upgrade works spark war zone complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Kuala Lumpur Hospital (HKL) has clarified that the crowded conditions at its Emergency and Trauma Department, described as a war zone on social media, are due to ongoing upgrading works. HKL director Datuk Dr Harikrishna K R Nair said the project is intended to increase patient capacity and improve comfort levels. Despite the space constraints, healthcare services at the Emergency and Trauma Department continue as usual under the no wrong door principle, he said in a statement.

items.title: Cattle caretaker 'grateful to be alive' after being shot in the face by thieves
items.summary: TANAH MERAH: A cattle caretaker shot in the face at close range by livestock thieves says he is simply grateful to be alive. Mohamad Khairi Amri Abdul Kadir, 42, sustained injuries to his right cheek and jaw while attempting to prevent a group from stealing cattle under his care. Recounting the 3am incident on April 20, Khairi said he spotted a group of men attempting to raid the cattle shed.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt rolls out 'Pace' plan to upskill workers, protect jobs [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will implement the Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment (Pace), a new economic resilience package aimed at strengthening Malaysia's labour market and workforce skills development. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the initiative, valued at more than RM710 million, will be carried out through the Human Resources Ministry and forms part of broader efforts under the Economic Resilience Package 2026. Anwar said Pace is part of a broader strategy to strengthen economic resilience, with significant implications for human capital development and labour market stability.

items.title: Japan warns yen speculators it stands ready to intervene again
items.summary: TOKYO: Japan's top currency diplomat Atsushi Mimura said on Friday that speculation remains rife, a blunt warning that Tokyo is ready to step back into markets after intervening just hours earlier to prop up the embattled yen. But I will tell you that Japan's Golden Week holidays have just started," Mimura told reporters when asked whether Tokyo could intervene in the currency market. Mimura declined to comment on whether Japan intervened in the currency market on Thursday.

items.title: Anwar announces RM710m PACE package to strengthen jobs and human capital development
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced the Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment (PACE) Economic Resilience Package, valued at more than RM710 million, as part of efforts to strengthen human capital development and ensure the stability of the national labour market. The Prime Minister said PACE forms part of the country’s economic resilience strategy. He also announced that the Self-Employment Social Security Act 2017 will be amended to expand protection under the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) to Malaysians working abroad, beginning with cross-border workers between Malaysia and Singapore.

items.title: Global economic conditions more challenging now than in February, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The government will continue to take the necessary measures in facing global economic uncertainty, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the global economic situation has become more challenging this month compared to three months ago in February. He said this when delivering his Workers’ Day message in conjunction with the National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration held here today.

items.title: Zambry: Education gaps start early, calls for holistic overhaul of Bumiputera education system
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir said educational gaps begin long before students enter university, calling for a holistic overhaul of Bumiputera education. He added that Bumiputera education must be approached in a comprehensive manner, spanning early childhood education, formal schooling, skills training, higher education, and a culture of lifelong learning. Among the key areas of focus are the review of Bumiputera education policies, mainstreaming Tahfiz education, strengthening Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), early childhood education, education endowment, and the future direction of national education.

items.title: Tourism minister urges critics to stop politicising Rain Rave festival, says it boosts economy and supports traders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing is calling on all parties to stop politicising the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF), which is currently taking place in Bukit Bintang. He said the programme aims to help spur the local economy while also giving everyone a chance to enjoy their Labour Day holiday. Tiong reiterated that RRWMF is not a Songkran festival, but a water-themed music festival, and reassured the public that visitor safety remains the top priority.

items.title: Storm damages over 30 homes and premises in Kepala Batas, no casualties reported
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, April 30 — More than 30 homes and business premises around Kepala Batas were reported damaged following a storm and strong winds this evening. John Sagun said the Kepala Batas Fire and Rescue Station was also affected when its roof was blown off by strong winds. Apart from Kepala Batas Hospital, similar incidents were also reported in several other areas around Penaga and Kepala Batas,” he said.

items.title: Over 15,000 expected at National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration in Bukit Jalil today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — More than 15,000 workers from the public and private sectors nationwide are expected to gather at the National Workers’ Day 2026 celebration at Unifi Arena, Bukit Jalil today. The celebration, themed “Pekerja Madani Kesuma Bangsa”, will be officiated by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, who is also scheduled to deliver a special address. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan is also expected to attend.

items.title: Tiong says Malaysia’s tourist arrivals continue to grow despite Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Tourism, Arts and Culture Minister Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said tourist arrivals in Malaysia continue to grow despite the Middle East conflict. Tiong said this during a press conference with local media following the launch of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF) at Bukit Bintang last night. “Up until today, our tourist numbers continue to grow, thank God for helping us.

items.title: Police arrest four foreigners in Seremban over alleged obscene video scam syndicate
items.summary: SEREMBAN, May 1 — Police detained four Chinese nationals during a raid here on April 23 for suspected involvement in an online scam syndicate selling obscene videos. Seremban Deputy Police Chief Supt Ahmad Lutfti Abdul Mutalip said the suspects, aged between 25 and 40, were apprehended by a team from the District Police Headquarters (IPD) Commercial Crime Investigation Division and the Negeri Sembilan Contingent Police Headquarters Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID). He said police seized four computers, 19 mobile phones of various brands, a router, a set of keys, and a remote control during the raid at a house.

items.title: Over 7,000 turn up for Rain Rave day one, KL police chief warns public against misconduct
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The opening of the Rain Rave Water Music Festival (RRWMF), held in conjunction with Visit Malaysia Year 2026, saw a massive turnout with over 7,000 attendees gathered in the heart of the city. “Numerous roads are currently closed. Please pay close attention to official traffic notices and plan your journeys accordingly to avoid congestion.

items.title: Clearer guidelines needed on cargo handling after Hin Leong, Ocean Tankers scandals, says industry player
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The maritime industry is urging regulators to provide clearer guidance on cargo handling practices, particularly involving oil commingling in shore tanks, following key lessons from past corporate failures in Singapore. “The cases exposed weaknesses in reconciling physical oil stocks with paper records across trading, storage and vessel operations, underscoring broader risks related to inventory verification, documentation integrity and traceability within the petroleum supply chain,” he said. He added that without proper alignment between physical cargo conditions and documentation, the industry risks ongoing compliance gaps.

items.title: Johor police chief: Two army personnel arrested over assault that left soldier with fractured skull
items.summary: MERSING, May 1 — Police have arrested two army personnel to assist in investigations into an incident that left a soldier seriously injured, including a fractured skull, at Kem Iskandar, here. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the two suspects, both 24 and from the same camp, were arrested by the Criminal Investigation Division of the Mersing district police headquarters at about 11.45 pm on March 14. Ab Rahaman said checks found that the suspects had no prior criminal records, and both tested negative for drugs.

items.title: Anthony Loke says decision on proposed diesel road tax reduction to be announced soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — The decision on the proposed reduction of road tax for diesel-powered vehicles will be announced soon. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the ministry is reviewing a suitable mechanism, including evaluating any potential impacts on government revenue. Last Saturday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government is considering lowering road tax on diesel vehicles to help ease people’s burden amid rising fuel prices.

items.title: Northern entry points at risk of smuggling should be led by police, says border agency chief
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 1 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) has proposed that all entry points in the north of Peninsular Malaysia at risk of being exploited for smuggling activities be led by senior police officers. AKPS director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said the agency required an Entry Point Commander who was well-versed in transborder crime and capable of acting swiftly to strengthen operational capabilities at the relevant entry points. Mohd Shuhaily also expressed appreciation to the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) for its swift action in a shooting incident involving the AKPS Bukit Kayu Hitam commander early this year.

items.title: Ramanan: Economic growth under Madani govt must go hand in hand with workers’ welfare
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — Every policy introduced under the Madani government to strengthen economic growth and national development will prioritise social justice and workers’ welfare, said Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan. He said workers are the backbone of the nation’s development, carrying values, dignity and roles that must not be overlooked. Meanwhile, he said Malaysia continues to show resilience in facing global challenges by adapting quickly and implementing responsive policies.

items.title: Explained: How police remand works and why suspects cannot be publicly identified before facing official charges
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 1 — In the hours following the apprehension of a suspect, before any formal charges are filed, a court process determines whether the person in custody can be held longer to facilitate ongoing police investigations. This formal process — a remand proceeding — is an important part of the Malaysian criminal justice system in which magistrates decide if police have sufficient grounds to continue detaining a suspect. A person is therefore “remanded” when the magistrate issues a remand order to extend the suspect’s detention beyond 24 hours.

items.title: Communications Ministry pledges vigilance, warns against misinformation as Gaza aid flotilla crisis unfolds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the government strongly condemns the interception of vessels under the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission by Israeli forces in international waters en route to Gaza. Fahmi said he has received a detailed briefing from Datuk Dr Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby, Director‑General of the Sumud Nusantara Command Centre (SNCC), on the incident involving 22 vessels. Fahmi noted that the incident also involved 10 Malaysian citizens.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan drama settles: Zahid confirms 14 Umno‑BN assemblymen staying with Unity pact
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 30 — All 14 assemblymen from Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) in Negeri Sembilan have agreed to continue supporting the state Unity Government to ensure political stability, said Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. The Umno president said the decision was reached following a discussion session held with all the representatives involved today. In addition, Ahmad Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said that as the country is currently facing global economic challenges, all parties must remain united in supporting Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s efforts to ensure political stability.

items.title: ‘Entertainment has limits’: Jawi calls Rain Rave immoral, issues advisory against festival
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 30 — The Federal Territories Islamic Religious Department (Jawi) has objected to the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 scheduled to be held in the capital, citing concerns it could harm moral standards and social norms. He also referred to Bayan Linnas (religious advisory) Series 351 titled “Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026: Entertainment Has Limits, Organisers Bear Responsibility and Immoral Concerts Must Be Stopped”, issued by the Federal Territories Mufti Department, which states that Islam does not reject entertainment outright but requires it to comply with syariah guidelines. He said Jawi will continue organising religious programmes, including lectures, talks, forums, tausiyah (religious reminder) sessions, social media campaigns and Friday sermons, to remind Muslims to adhere to Allah SWT’s commands and avoid activities deemed immoral.

items.title: Health minister assures no cuts to treatment, emergency care under Budget 2026 reprioritisation
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 30 — The government’s adjustment to operating expenditure will not affect essential healthcare services, including treatment, emergency care and the supply of medicines, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. It has not been finalised,” he told reporters after launching the Malaysia Occupational Health Services for Public Sector (MyOHS-PS) and Integrated Workplace Health Programmes (IWHP) here today. Dzulkefly said the government remains committed to safeguarding core healthcare services as outlined in Budget 2026.
